description: Triton Inference Server client + service infrastructure. Add a new package @quant/triton-client that wraps Triton HTTP/gRPC inference protocol. Replace the naive neural-cf.ts with a Triton-backed client. Add Triton to docker-compose and Helm values. Add model repository structure for ONNX models.
steps: Create packages/triton-client/ with package.json (deps: zod), tsconfig.json, src/index.ts barrel export

Create src/triton-http-client.ts: TritonInferenceClient class that speaks Triton HTTP inference protocol v2 (POST /v2/models/{model}/infer). Interface-based with TritonTransport for DI/mocking. Methods: infer(modelName, inputs) -> outputs, getModelStatus(modelName), getServerHealth(). Input/output typed with TensorData (name, shape, datatype, data).
Create src/triton-model-registry.ts: ModelRegistry class that tracks available models, their input/output schemas, and version info. Methods: registerModel(), getModel(), listModels().
Create src/types.ts with all Triton protocol types: InferRequest, InferResponse, TensorData, ModelMetadata, ServerHealthResponse, etc. Validate with Zod schemas.
Create src/__tests__/triton-http-client.test.ts: mock the transport layer, test inference calls serialize correctly, test error handling, test health check, test batch inference.

Create packages/recommendations/src/core/neural-cf.ts as a new TritonNCFClient class: wraps @quant/triton-client to call the 'neural-cf' model on Triton. Constructor takes TritonInferenceClient. predict(userId, itemId) encodes inputs as tensors, calls infer, returns score. recommend(userId, candidates, topN) batches predictions. Export maintains same public API shape (predict, recommend, getUserEmbedding, getItemEmbedding) but delegates to Triton. Has a fallback mode using the in-memory fixture for local dev.
Update packages/recommendations/src/index.ts to export the new NeuralCF (it already does, just ensure types align).

Add triton service to docker-compose.dev.yml: image nvcr.io/nvidia/tritonserver:24.01-py3, ports 8000:8000 (HTTP) 8001:8001 (gRPC) 8002:8002 (metrics), volume mount ./models:/models, command tritonserver --model-repository=/models --strict-model-config=false
Create models/ directory at repo root with models/neural-cf/1/model.onnx placeholder (create a README explaining how to export), models/neural-cf/config.pbtxt for Triton model config.
Add triton-inference-server service to infra/helm/quant-platform/values.yaml following the pattern of existing services (enabled, replicaCount, image, port, resources, autoscaling, probes).
Update pnpm-workspace.yaml if needed to include packages/triton-client.
Ensure pnpm install, typecheck, test, build, lint all pass for the new package.
acceptance_criteria: packages/triton-client/ builds and passes typecheck with no errors
TritonInferenceClient test suite passes with mocked transport (at least 5 test cases)
packages/recommendations/src/core/neural-cf.ts now uses Triton client (not in-memory Math.random)
Helm values.yaml includes triton-inference-server service entry
pnpm typecheck passes for entire monorepo
findings: All acceptance criteria met. triton-client builds/typechecks with 9 passing tests (mocked transport). recommendations package has 67 passing tests with new Triton-backed NeuralCF. Prettier ran via lint-staged hook on commit with no issues. The NeuralCF public API is now async (returns Promises) since it calls Triton over HTTP. Fallback mode with InMemoryNeuralCF is synchronous but wrapped in the async interface.
